ff3466f1-5428-4c0e-ac12-1529c9b81cba.label: text_chunk
ff3466f1-5428-4c0e-ac12-1529c9b81cba.text:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

PARTIES : McMaster University (‘ University ’) 1280 Main Street West Hamilton,

 ON  L8S 4L8
Company/Organization name (‘ Company ’) Company/organization address

DATE: March 12,

32a4ddd9-2414-4c8c-ace4-4ff3fd2a45d8.label: text_chunk
32a4ddd9-2414-4c8c-ace4-4ff3fd2a45d8.text:
2019 (‘Effective Date’)

In consideration of the mutual covenants set out in this Agreement and for other good and valuable consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged by each of the Parties), the Parties agree as follows:

1.

fc2141c5-bae5-4f2e-8cba-3ef01e1ee85b.label: text_chunk
fc2141c5-bae5-4f2e-8cba-3ef01e1ee85b.text:
DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

Confidential Inf ormation means any information disclosed by one party (the ‘ Discloser ’) to the other (the ‘ Recipient ’) relating directly or indirectly to Name of Technology/Project, file # which is identified by the Discloser, either orally or in writing, as confidential, either at the time of disclosure or, if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing within thirty (30) days following the original disclosure.

2.

d75dea5e-210a-44f3-bbb9-0b8f491fb02f.label: text_chunk
d75dea5e-210a-44f3-bbb9-0b8f491fb02f.text:
EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

This Agreement does not apply to information that:

i.  was available to the public at the time of disclosure,

 or subsequently became available to the public without fault of Recipient;

ii.

  was known to Recipient at the time of disclosure or was independently developed by Recipient, provided there is adequate documentation to confirm such prior knowledge or independent development;

iii.

5c207a4e-9175-4c32-8410-8d74e92df16e.label: text_chunk

b5fb0a71-435a-429c-975e-bcf36459c0fc.label: text_chunk
b5fb0a71-435a-429c-975e-bcf36459c0fc.text:
Any action taken by Discloser to contest the disclosure must not compromise the obligations of Recipient under the order to disclose or cause Recipient to be subject to any fine, penalty or prosecution.

3.

0fd7d986-cb8b-4492-ae1b-9c255812cdbe.label: text_chunk
0fd7d986-cb8b-4492-ae1b-9c255812cdbe.text:
DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES

Each party designates a representative for coordinating receipt, release and delivery of Confidential Information, which for the University will be Name of Principal Investigator and/or Technology Transfer Office representative and for Company: Name of designated representative for company, or another individual(s) as the party may designate in writing to the other party.

4. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

4.

1dd451fc-3e24-4b67-b9c2-a2ca40afade0.label: text_chunk
1dd451fc-3e24-4b67-b9c2-a2ca40afade0.text:
USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Recipient may only use the Confidential Information for the purpose of type of purpose/or a research collaboration and not for any commercial purpose (‘ Permitted Purpose ’). Recipient must not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose without the prior written approval of Discloser.

5. NON-DISCLOSURE

Recipient must keep the Confidential Information in confidence.

07945271-1a1c-4dca-8c6a-ae29b34c216e.label: text_chunk
07945271-1a1c-4dca-8c6a-ae29b34c216e.text:
Recipient may only disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, directors, officers, agents, students (in University’s case) and
consultants who have a need-to-know the Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose, provided that they are advised of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality. Recipient must not otherwise disclose Confidential Information to any person or third party without the prior written approval of Discloser.

6.

b74d6432-6076-49b7-b577-81486af564d1.label: text_chunk
b74d6432-6076-49b7-b577-81486af564d1.text:
STANDARD OF CARE

Recipient must use at least the same standard of care in protecting the confidentiality of the Confidential Information as it uses in protecting its own information of a similar nature and, in any event, no less than a reasonable standard of care. Recipient must notify Discloser promptly upon discovery that any Confidential Information has been accessed or otherwise acquired by or disclosed to an unauthorized person.

7.

b4837040-1861-43e5-a3be-af6a8056ec1a.label: text_chunk
b4837040-1861-43e5-a3be-af6a8056ec1a.text:
RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

If requested in writing by Discloser, Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possession or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, provided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confidentiality requirements set out in this Agreement.

8.

12795ae0-37fe-4e5c-ba76-ac61b85127c5.label: text_chunk
12795ae0-37fe-4e5c-ba76-ac61b85127c5.text:
NO LICENCE OR OTHER RIGHTS

All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any disclosure of Confidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.

9. LIMITED WARRANTY & LIABILITY

Discloser warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to Recipient.

96072870-a820-4b8e-8ad3-ff0209c32bd7.label: text_chunk
96072870-a820-4b8e-8ad3-ff0209c32bd7.text:
Discloser makes no other warranties in respect of the Confidential Information and provides all information “AS IS” without any express or implied warranty of any kind, including any warranty as to merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, completeness or violation of third party intellectual property rights.

 Neither party will be liable for any special, incidental or consequential damages of any kind whatsoever resulting from the disclosure, use or receipt of the Confidential Information.

10.

bd6c3c80-6e67-4075-899f-0793f6578eb1.label: text_chunk
bd6c3c80-6e67-4075-899f-0793f6578eb1.text:
TERM

This Agreement and Recipient’s obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential expires three (3) years after the Effective Date.

11. GENERAL PROVISIONS

11.

1 Notices - All notices given under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered by courier or registered mail, return receipt requested, or facsimile, to the address of the party set out on page one of this Agreement.

80b8b804-72e7-4aa6-84b8-05b7c460102b.label: text_chunk
80b8b804-72e7-4aa6-84b8-05b7c460102b.text:
All notices to the University must be addressed to:
Executive Director McMaster Industry Liaison Office 175 Longwood Road South McMaster Innovation Park, Rm. 305 Hamilton, ON  L8P 0A1 Tel. 905.525.9140, ext.

 23164 Fax. 905.546.1372 yuyitun@mcmaster.ca
and all notices to the Company must be addressed to:

title/contact name for position.

8ac08c08-5ca7-4d78-af17-d67b2a2f2b55.label: text_chunk
8ac08c08-5ca7-4d78-af17-d67b2a2f2b55.text:
Notices will be deemed to have been received on the date of delivery, if delivered by courier, on the fifth business day following receipt, if delivered by registered mail or on the first business day following the electronic confirmation of the successful transmission of the facsimile, if sent by facsimile.

11.

2 Remedies - Recipient agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach or threat ened breach of the Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.

1854782b-31a2-4986-8bbe-fe46354c9257.label: text_chunk
1854782b-31a2-4986-8bbe-fe46354c9257.text:
Accordingly, in addition to any and all other available remedies, Discloser will be entitled to seek a temporary or permanent injunction or any other form of equitable relief to enforce the obligations contained in this Agreement.

11.

3 No waiver – Failure of a party to enforce its rights on one occasion will not result in a waiver of those rights on any other occasion.

11.

4 Assignment - Neither party may assign any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.

58b33fac-b502-4312-a410-a496a5c6cac8.label: text_chunk
58b33fac-b502-4312-a410-a496a5c6cac8.text:
11.5 Regulatory compliance – Each party must comply with all applicable laws,

 regulations and rules in its jurisdiction, including but not limited to those relating to the export of information and data.

11.

6 Entire Agreement – This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the parties with regard to the Confidential Information and supersedes any previous understandings, commitments or agreements, whether written or oral. No amendment or modification of this Agreement will be effective unless made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

54bebdc7-9fd0-418b-b675-d6269dfc93d9.label: text_chunk
54bebdc7-9fd0-418b-b675-d6269dfc93d9.text:
11.

7 Severability – If any provision of this Agreement is wholly or partially unenforceable for any reason, all other provisions will continue in full force and effect.

11.

8 Binding Effect - This Agreement is binding upon and will enure to the benefits of the parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

11.

f7f70a84-4bfe-40db-8d9f-a8e04cc22dba.label: text_chunk
f7f70a84-4bfe-40db-8d9f-a8e04cc22dba.text:
9 Execution - This Agreement may be executed by the parties in counterparts and may be executed and delivered by facsimile or electronically by PDF and all such counterparts, facsimiles and PDF copies shall together constitute one agreement.  The parties agree that facsimile or PDF copies of signatures have the same effect as original signatures.

11.

feb1d284-7c81-49d5-99ec-652f1aab8ae7.label: text_chunk
feb1d284-7c81-49d5-99ec-652f1aab8ae7.text: 10 Governing Law - This Agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario and the laws of Canada and the parties attorn to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Province of Ontario.The parties have duly executed this Agreement by their duly authorized representatives as of the Effective Date.UNIVERSITY COMPANY __________________________ __________________________ Gay Yuyitung Name of signatory Executive Director, MILO Title __________________________ __________________________ Date Date

community_summary.0: The relationships highlight a formal connection between McMaster University and a Company through a Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA). Both entities are recognized as parties to this agreement, which signifies a commitment to confidentiality and collaboration. This relationship underscores the importance of protecting sensitive information exchanged between McMaster University and the Company, reflecting a structured partnership aimed at fostering trust and cooperation.
community_summary.1: In the context of an agreement, the 'Parties' involved recognize the 'Effective Date' as the crucial moment when the terms of the agreement become applicable. This mutual acknowledgment signifies the importance of this date in establishing the validity and enforceability of the agreement's provisions.
community_summary.2:
The relationships outlined involve a central entity, the Discloser, and its interactions with the Recipient and the Agreement. 

1. **Discloser and Recipient**: The Discloser shares confidential information with the Recipient, establishing a relationship based on trust and confidentiality. This relationship is reinforced by the expectation that the Recipient will maintain the confidentiality of the information received. The Discloser provides this information under specific terms outlined in an agreement, emphasizing the legal framework governing their interaction.

2. **Contesting Disclosure**: The Discloser also has the right to contest any disclosures made by the Recipient, indicating a dynamic where the Discloser actively engages in protecting its confidential information and holds the Recipient accountable for any obligations to disclose.

3. **Seeking Remedies**: In cases where the Discloser's rights are threatened, it is entitled to seek remedies as specified in the Agreement. This includes the possibility of obtaining injunctions to enforce its obligations, highlighting the legal recourse available to the Discloser in maintaining the integrity of the confidential relationship.

Overall, the relationships emphasize a structured interaction where confidentiality, trust, and legal obligations are paramount, with the Discloser maintaining significant control over the information shared and the conditions under which it is disclosed.
community_summary.3: The relationship between "Confidential Information" and "Name of Technology/Project" is characterized by a direct association where the confidential information is integral to the understanding and functioning of the specific technology or project. This relationship underscores the importance of safeguarding sensitive information that is essential for the development and implementation of the technology or project in question.
community_summary.4:
The relationships summarized below highlight the key aspects of an Agreement and its implications for the involved entities:

1. **Agreement and Recipient**: The Agreement outlines the terms for the Recipient's handling of confidential information, ensuring that the Recipient understands their responsibilities regarding confidentiality.

2. **Agreement and Confidential Information**: The Agreement governs the sharing and protection of Confidential Information between the Discloser and the Recipient, establishing clear guidelines for both parties.

3. **Agreement and Effective Date**: The Effective Date signifies the commencement of the obligations specified in the Agreement, marking when the terms become enforceable.

4. **Agreement and Notices**: The Agreement includes provisions that detail how and when notices are delivered to the Recipient, ensuring proper communication between the parties.

5. **Agreement and Parties**: The Agreement is binding upon all Parties involved, meaning that they are legally obligated to adhere to the terms and conditions set forth in the document.

6. **Agreement and Involvement of Parties**: The Agreement serves as a formal document that binds the Parties to its terms, emphasizing the importance of compliance and mutual understanding.

Overall, the Agreement serves as a critical framework for managing confidential information, establishing obligations, and ensuring effective communication between the involved Parties.
community_summary.5:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Recipient and Discloser**: The Recipient is legally bound by a Confidentiality Agreement established by the Discloser, which mandates the protection of Confidential Information. This relationship emphasizes the Recipient's obligation to maintain confidentiality and adhere to the terms set forth by the Discloser.

2. **Approval Requirement**: The Recipient must seek prior written approval from the Discloser before using or disclosing any Confidential Information, highlighting a dependent relationship that requires consent from the Discloser.

3. **Use of Confidential Information**: The Recipient is permitted to use the Confidential Information solely for specific purposes as defined in the agreement, indicating a controlled and limited use of sensitive data.

4. **Receiving Confidential Information**: Upon receiving Confidential Information, the Recipient is obligated to keep it confidential, reinforcing the trust placed by the Discloser in the Recipient.

5. **Handling Responsibilities**: The Recipient is responsible for managing the Confidential Information in accordance with the confidentiality requirements outlined in the agreement, ensuring proper care and protection of sensitive data.

6. **Obligations and Consequences**: The Recipient has a clear obligation to maintain confidentiality as stipulated in the Agreement, which also details the consequences of any breaches, underscoring the seriousness of the commitment.

Overall, the relationships illustrate a structured framework of confidentiality where the Recipient is entrusted with sensitive information under strict conditions set by the Discloser, emphasizing legal and ethical responsibilities.

community_summary.13:
The relationships outlined involve the University Company, Gay Yuyitung, and the Province of Ontario. 

1. **Gay Yuyitung and University Company**: Gay Yuyitung serves as the authorized signatory for the University Company, which means he represents the organization in legal matters. This role is crucial as it designates him as the official point of contact for legal agreements and responsibilities.

2. **University Company and Province of Ontario**: The operations of the University Company are governed by the laws of the Province of Ontario. This establishes a legal framework that guides the company's activities and ensures compliance with regional regulations.

In summary, Gay Yuyitung is the legal representative of the University Company, while the company's operations are regulated under the jurisdiction of the Province of Ontario.
community_summary.14:
The relationships highlight the role of the Principal Investigator (PI) in the context of a University and the management of Confidential Information. 

1. **Principal Investigator and University**: The Principal Investigator is affiliated with the University, where they oversee research projects and ensure compliance with institutional agreements. This relationship underscores the PI's leadership role in academic research and governance.

2. **Principal Investigator and Confidential Information**: The Principal Investigator is responsible for managing Confidential Information, ensuring that faculty and staff understand and adhere to confidentiality obligations. This emphasizes the PI's duty to protect sensitive information and maintain ethical standards within the research environment.

Overall, the summary illustrates the dual responsibilities of the Principal Investigator in both academic oversight and the management of confidential data, highlighting their critical role in maintaining integrity and compliance within the research framework of the University.
community_summary.15: The Gripen aircraft is central to the Gripen Demonstrator Programme, which is dedicated to its ongoing development and evaluation. This relationship highlights the significance of the Gripen aircraft in advancing its capabilities through the structured framework of the programme.
community_summary.16:
The relationships outlined involve two entities referred to as "Party" and their interactions concerning Confidential Information and a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA). 

1. **Disclosure of Confidential Information**: The Party is responsible for disclosing Confidential Information to the other Party as stipulated in their agreement. This emphasizes the obligation of one Party to share sensitive information with the other.

2. **Obligations under the NDA**: Both Parties are bound by the terms of the NDA, which outlines their rights and responsibilities regarding the handling of Confidential Information. This highlights the legal framework that governs their relationship and ensures protection of sensitive data.

In summary, the Parties are engaged in a formal agreement where one Party discloses Confidential Information, and both Parties are legally bound by an NDA that regulates their interactions and responsibilities concerning that information.
community_summary.17: The relationship between "Conditional Access" and "Information" is characterized by a restriction mechanism. Conditional Access serves to limit the type of Information that can be accessed, contingent upon adherence to specific compliance agreements. This highlights the importance of compliance in determining access rights to sensitive information.
community_summary.18: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) plays a crucial role in national security by enforcing Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs). These agreements are mandated to ensure that individuals or entities safeguard sensitive information pertinent to national security. The enforcement of NDAs by the DHS underscores the importance of confidentiality in protecting national interests.

2. **Section 1034 of Title 10** and the **Military Whistleblower Protection Act**: These entities are focused on safeguarding individuals who report misconduct. Section 1034 specifically protects military personnel, while the Military Whistleblower Protection Act offers broader protections applicable to various disclosures.

Overall, these relationships highlight the legal frameworks designed to protect employee rights and ensure the safety of whistleblowers, particularly in military contexts.

The relationship between the Act and the amendment highlights the importance of legal frameworks in supporting whistleblower rights.

The relationships outlined highlight the significant impact of the Woolsey Fire on various infrastructure projects in the area. 

1. **Guardrail Replacement Citywide**: This project was initiated due to damage caused by the Woolsey Fire, which affected guardrails throughout the city.

2. **Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs**: The storm drain on Latigo Canyon Road suffered damage from the Woolsey Fire, prompting the need for repairs.

3. **Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements**: Similar to the previous projects, this initiative is a direct response to the damage inflicted by the Woolsey Fire on drainage systems.

Overall, the Woolsey Fire has been a catalyst for multiple infrastructure repair and improvement projects, specifically targeting damaged guardrails and drainage systems in the affected areas.
